title: Record flashy demo video clip of Neovim config features for README
description: Create a short screen-recorded demo clip showcasing the config's most visual/flashy features (e.g. dashboard, statusline, completion menu, fuzzy finder/Telescope, file tree, LSP/diagnostics, treesitter highlighting, any custom UI/animations). Embed it in the README so visitors see the config in action at a glance. Mirrors the demo-clip approach used in other repos.
acceptance_criteria:
- Short clip (~15-45s) recorded showing several flashy features in a real editing session
- Exported as lightweight format suitable for README (GIF or mp4/webm via GitHub asset)
- Embedded/linked at top of README.md
- Clip loads/plays on the repo's git host
notes: Scope: full neovim + tmux setup fair game for the demo (panes/splits, tmux statusline, session switching) — keep it to the normal everyday config, nothing too crazy/experimental. Tools: vhs (.tape, reproducible scripted demo, handles tmux), or asciinema+agg for terminal GIF, or OBS/wf-recorder for mp4. vhs best fit for a config repo.
close_reason: Recorded demo GIF (recording/ via VHS) and embedded at top of README
